reasoning: The 200m freestyle long course world record (1:42.00 set by Paul Biedermann in 2009) is notoriously difficult to break, as it was set during the polyurethane "super-suit" era. As the text confirms he still holds it as of mid-October 2025, it is extremely unlikely to fall between November 2025 and January 2026. This period is traditionally the short course season (SCM), meaning elite swimmers are typically competing in 25m pools or engaged in heavy off-season training, rather than tapering for a monumental long course personal best. Furthermore, any retroactive disqualifications for 2009 are prevented by the WADA 10-year statute of limitations. The probabilities slightly decrease over time to reflect naturally compounding uncertainty, but remain capped near the maximum allowed confidence.
